options.name: -Command
options.description: Deletes jobs that include the specified words in the command. You can enter a comma-separated array.

options.name: -Filter
options.description: Deletes jobs that satisfy all the conditions established in the associated hash table. Enter a hash table where the keys are job properties and the values are job property values.

name: -Force
-F
options.description: Deletes a job even if the job's state is Running . If the Force parameter isn't specified, `Remove-Job` doesn't delete running jobs.

options.name: -Id
options.description: Deletes background jobs with the specified Id . You can enter a comma-separated array. The job's Id is a unique integer that identifies a job within the current session.

options.name: -InstanceId
options.description: Deletes jobs with the specified InstanceId . You can enter a comma-separated array. An InstanceId is a unique GUID that identifies a job.

options.name: -Job
options.description: Specifies the jobs to be deleted. Enter a variable that contains the jobs or a command that gets the jobs. You can enter a comma-separated array.

options.name: -Name
options.description: Only deletes jobs with the specified friendly name. Wildcards are permitted. You can enter a comma-separated array.

options.name: -State
options.description: Only deletes jobs with the specified state. To delete jobs with a state of Running , use the Force parameter.

name: -Confirm
-cf
options.description: Prompts you for confirmation before `Remove-Job` is run.

name: -WhatIf
-wi
options.description: Shows what would happen if `Remove-Job` runs. The cmdlet isn't run.
